rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Proposed Supplemental Petition To Quiet Title
rulings.Rulings: The Section 850 Petition and Elder Abuse action filed by Henry Stevens is set on the Master Calendar for trial on January 7, 2008. Settlement conference is set for December 17, 2007. The Probate Department cannot determine the form or extent of pleadings in a matter that has been set for trial on the Master Calendar. Any change of pleadings or related motions must be set for hearing in the Law and Motion Department. The Probate Department therefore will not rule on Henry Stevens' Supplemental Petition or Yolanda Paul's Motion to Strike. The only matters remaining for hearing before the Probate Department are the Petition for Probate of Will filed by Yolanda Paul and the Petition for Letters of Administration filed by Henry Stevens. Both of these probate petitions are set on the Probate Department calendar of January 23, 2008 at 9:00 a.m.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Ex Parte Petition For Leave To File Amendment To Supplemental Petition Adding Doe Defendant
rulings.Rulings: The Probate Department will not rule on Henry Stevens' ex parte Petition for Leave to File Amendment to Supplemental Petition. The underlying petition has been set for trial on the Master Calendar. Any request for leave to file amendment must be heard in the Law and Motion Department.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE BENUSTO BARNACHEA NAPENAS LIVING TRUST DATED JANUARY 20, 2006 (TRUST), WHICH BECAME IRREVOCABLE ON THE DEA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Zosima Bumatay Mulato Dizon To Strike Trust Contest, Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Tentative ruling: 1. Motion to Strike amended petition for lack of verification is denied. 2. Motion to Strike pursuant to CCP Section 425.16 is denied. 3. Motion to Strike references to purported will is granted in part and denied in part. Page 2 line 26 through page 3 line 2, and page 2 lines 5 through 11 are stricken as irrelevant. Motion is denied as to other references to the purported will. 4. Although the Court exercises its discretion to consider respondent's late filed opposition, the opposition is stricken as to page 3 line 31 through page 4 line 11, page 4 line 31 through page 5 line 16, and Exhibit D as irrelevant and improper. 5. No sanctions are ordered against either party at this time.
